paper_title: An Approach to Quantify Endomembrane Dynamics in Pollen Utilizing Bioactive Chemicals

methods.description: This method involves analyzing images to quantify RAB2:GFP bodies in pollen grains using the Imaris software. The process includes segmentation, tracking, and statistical analysis to determine various parameters such as area, velocity, and intensity of the bodies.

steps.description: Images were analyzed using Imaris (version 7.3.1, Bitplane, South Windsor, CT) with the 'spots' software package.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze images using Imaris software with 'spots' package

steps.description: RAB2:GFP bodies were quantified using the automatic threshold function provided by the 'spots' algorithm builder.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify RAB2:GFP bodies using automatic threshold function

steps.description: Segmentation along with region growing was used to quantify the fluorescent RAB2:GFP bodies.
steps.searchable_summary: use segmentation and region growing to quantify fluorescent bodies

steps.description: The average area of three replicates was recorded in μm².
steps.searchable_summary: record average area of three replicates

steps.description: Three biological replicates were used in quantifying the average speed of RAB2:GFP bodies using the 'spots' tracking function.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify average speed using 'spots' tracking function

steps.description: An additional filter included in the spots package was used to exclude those bodies that were unable to be tracked more than 15 frames.
steps.searchable_summary: apply filter to exclude bodies tracked less than 15 frames

steps.description: Area, velocity, straightness, and intensity were represented as cumulative distribution plots.
steps.searchable_summary: create cumulative distribution plots for area, velocity, straightness, and intensity

steps.description: The mean and standard deviation for all spots in a movie were calculated and used in determining the z score.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean, standard deviation, and z score for spots

steps.description: This z score, also called the CDF%, was plotted against the average of the selected parameter.
steps.searchable_summary: plot z score (CDF%) against average parameter

steps.description: From these average values, a difference index was created.
steps.searchable_summary: create difference index from average values
